subject: Re: M may not give expected results
body:
Hi Nicolas,
When you see something like this there are a few possibilities
1. data not strong enough for refinement of the more complex models used in M
2. Something wrong with the project/import means the refinement is looking for particles in the wrong place
For 1. I often see people trying to refine time resolved poses and all parameters with high resolution grids straight away - I tend to start with a simpler model and add more when refinement stops improving. Everything is prone to overfitting with weak data.
For 2. I would do some sanity checks, export subtomos from M and average them the same way you would in warp to check your species has imported successfully 
Cheers!
Alister
Sent from mobile - apologies for brevity
On 30 Aug 2023, at 19:25, Nicolas Coudray <coudrn01@nyu.edu> wrote:
﻿
Hi -
 I have been trying to run M (1.1.0 beta)  on a windows station with 4 RTX 3090 GPUs (cuda 12.2), but the sub-tomograms generated lead to a refinement with worse resolution than before going through M. 
 Looking more into the star file generated, I noticed the X,Y,Z coordinates of the sub-tomograms were changing dramatically. I would expect the coordinate in the output file to be equal to something like
"(_rlnCoordinateX - _rlnOriginXAngst x PixelSize) x scaling_factor"  
with scaling factor the difference between the pixelsize of the star file given to M and the one generated after refinement in M. 
 To try to debug, I imported a star file, and then immediately exported exported sub-tomograms without refinement at the same pixel size as the input one. I noticed the coordinates of the first sub-tomogram was as expected, but all the others I checked had X,Y,Z, coodinates which were significantly different from those in the input file. 
 Has anyone any idea? I've seen other threads mentioning various issues with RTX 3090, so I was wondering if it could be some configuration / compatibility issue? Shall we downgrade to an older version of cuda (if so, which one would you advise)?
Thanks,
Nicolas
